title: JPG Screenshot
description: Saves screenshots as JPG files, reduce file size by about 80%
body:
## JPG Screenshot has a new look!

![old icon](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f495acd817136fae999dae3132e7f371ceafddbb.png) ![right arrow](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e68e367ef24e2d3e337db66a3c806621ab4c01b2_0.webp) ![new icon](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/d97f9e379813666d48d7f0e0dd2f7441e9658a97_0.webp)

New icon, same old two-mixin mod. Now updated for 26.1!

## Why save screenshots as jpg?

### Save disk space!

Minecraft saves screenshots as ~2MB png files at 1080p. This mod changes it to ~400KB high quality jpg files which look almost indistinguishable.

Perfect if you:
- Take lots of screenshots
- Can't let them go
- Don't want to buy another harddisk

**Small downside:** saving the screenshot is slightly longer, although the screenshot itself is instantaneous. Taking many screenshots in succession may cause the game to stutter.

## Can I still take png screenshots?

Yes, just hold **Left CTRL Key** while taking a screenshot. You can change this key in the game's Key Binds setting.

### About older versions

Prior to version 2.0.0, the default modifier key was Left Alt Key. Also, the jpg quality used to be worse.

## Issues

If you find an issue, feel free to [open an issue here](https://github.com/spiralhalo/JpgMcScreenshot/issues/new).

### Other things

Fabric API is required to properly display the keybind label and modmenu description.
